description_long: This house features a prominent gable roof with reddish-brown shingles and two visible chimneys. The front facade is distinguished by a central bay window with large glass panes, flanked by brick walls and a covered entryway. The exterior is primarily composed of red brick with white trim around the windows and roof edges, giving it a classic suburban appearance. There is a side carport or covered porch area, and the rear of the house includes a large glass wall, likely for a living or dining area. Windows are arranged asymmetrically, with a mix of rectangular and square shapes, and the house appears to have two stories. The overall design blends traditional and modern elements, making it suitable for a residential neighborhood.
description: A two-story brick house with a gable roof, bay window, and a mix of large and small windows, featuring a covered entry and carport.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bay window, main entry door, and a side carport with a brick facade and gable roof.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting a large glass wall under the gable roof and a covered porch area.
description_view_2: Alternate front angle with clear visibility of the bay window, entryway, and carport structure.
description_view_3: Side view displaying asymmetrical window placement and the full height of the two-story brick wall.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with bay window and large glass wall
attribute_entryway_design: Central bay window entry with glass double doors, covered by a gable overhang and flanked by brick columns
